rules.instruction: If files disagree, prefer ai/*.json first, then canonical root JSON files, then runtime CSS, then showcase pages.
rules.rationale: Showcase HTML can drift and may include manual exceptions.

rules.instruction: Default to dark theme unless the target context is proposal, report, print, or another readability-first editorial surface.
rules.rationale: The system is dark-first but has a defined light mode for document-heavy outputs.

rules.instruction: Use VT323 for display, pixel, and headline moments only.
rules.rationale: It is part of the brand signature and should not become body text.

rules.instruction: Use Inconsolata for body, UI, labels, captions, and most reading tasks.
Use at most one primary neon CTA per viewport or major section.
rules.rationale: Too many primary neon actions weaken hierarchy and brand discipline.

rules.instruction: In new implementation work, consume semantic tokens instead of primitive tokens.
rules.rationale: This preserves theme behavior and future maintainability.

rules.instruction: Prefer recipes before building one-off sections from scratch.
rules.rationale: Recipes carry the clearest brand signature and reduce subjective assembly.

rules.instruction: Prefer assets marked current. Do not start new work with legacy assets unless the task is explicitly legacy maintenance.
rules.rationale: Legacy assets exist for compatibility, not for new production.

rules.instruction: Only use icon ids that exist in ../assets/icons.svg.
rules.rationale: Legacy ids in historic docs do not guarantee runtime availability.

rules.instruction: Treat pages and examples as reference examples, not as canonical API or source of truth.
rules.rationale: Examples may contain inline styles and context-specific exceptions.
